text:
Market today: The market mood is cautious, driven by surging oil prices and growing fears of Federal Reserve rate hikes. This directly impacts your tech/chip heavy portfolio, as higher rates increase borrowing costs and reduce the appeal of growth stocks.

Your holdings: SOXL (-38.59%) and SPAL (-55.17%) are flashing red. Both are leveraged ETFs, meaning drawdowns are amplified, and they suffer from decay. SPAL is a new, very volatile leveraged ETF that's already taken a big hit. Your margin amplifies these drops. QQQI (-5.35%) is also a "SELL" and close to its stop.

Watch: Keep a close eye on interest rate expectations and oil price movements. Also, monitor your leveraged positions, especially SOXL and SPAL, for further downside.

Bottom line: Protect your capital in leveraged, underperforming positions as rate hike fears persist.
plays: SOXL: Your -38.59% loss is significant. Consider trimming or exiting this leveraged position if it continues to fail its stop at 200, given the rate hike environment.
SPAL: This new, highly volatile leveraged ETF is down -55.17%. Respect its stop at 21.38 to prevent further deep losses.
No new buys today - hold and watch: The market sentiment around rates and cost of capital suggests caution. Focus on risk management.
